easy.companyDescription: A consumer electronics company entering a fast-growing mobile-device category.
easy.macroContext: Mobile computing is moving beyond voice and text toward richer internet services.
easy.situation: Can a premium handheld product open a larger platform before carriers and incumbents shut the window?
easy.longCase: A loyal consumer base and strong design reputation could pull users into a new category and denser ecosystem.
easy.shortCase: Carriers and entrenched device makers still control distribution, and a launch miss would be costly after a strong run-up.
setupHints: The stock already prices meaningful execution, so a weak debut leaves little cushion.

medium.companyDescription: A branded consumer products company known for design-led hardware.
medium.macroContext: Better networks and data plans are changing what people expect from handheld devices.
medium.situation: Can product excellence convert into durable adoption when partners still control distribution?
medium.longCase: A loyal following could accept a premium approach and expand the addressable market.
medium.shortCase: Channel partners and entrenched rivals can slow scale, and the stock already prices a clean launch.

hard.macroContext: Device categories and partner distribution terms are being re-cut.
hard.situation: Is the market pricing a successful category entry that is still unproven at scale?
hard.longCase: Brand pull could accelerate adoption if the new product sticks with users.
hard.shortCase: Channel partners and incumbents still control scale, and expectations leave little room for a miss.

reveal.shortText: That was Apple. The iPhone launch quickly reframed the company as a mobile platform contender.
reveal.funFact: Apple sold over 6 million first-generation iPhones, but the App Store launched a year later is what truly unlocked the platform.
whyItMoved: The new handheld product gained rapid consumer attention.
Investors began pricing a broader platform opportunity.

sources.label: Prototype sample seed
sources.notes: Placeholder sample content for Phase 3 pipeline; not production research.

review.reviewNotes: H027 Hard rewrite after H026 Gate 2 identity fail (correct #1 conf 24). Shared-label exception: decisionDateLabel Jan 2007→2007; era Smartphone platform era→Consumer device transition (title Category Test kept). Hard prose removes portable/always-connected and premium-product-only cues. Peer set Apple/Nokia/RIM/Motorola/Sony/Palm. Easy/Medium prose preserved; all Gate 2 entries removed as stale. Follow-up blind judge must rejudge easy+medium+hard.

revealOnly: non-phone device base expanding into handhelds
decisionUseful: high launch expectations in the stock
